questions.question: Q1. Everybody in the office _______ ready to start the project.
questions.explanation: "Everybody" is singular and takes a singular verb "is". "Everybody" singular pronoun है इसलिए इसके साथ singular verb का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q2. If anybody calls while I am out, tell _______ that I will return shortly.
questions.explanation: "Anybody" is singular, so it should be followed by a singular pronoun like "he". "Anybody" singular है, इसलिए pronoun भी singular (he) होना चाहिए।

questions.question: Q3. Nothing but a few old chairs _______ found in the room.
questions.explanation: "Nothing" is the main subject here, which is singular, making "was" the correct verb. "Nothing" यहाँ main subject है जो singular है, इसलिए singular verb "was" लगेगा।

questions.question: Q4. Something _______ missing from the shelf since yesterday.
questions.explanation: "Something" is a singular subject and takes the singular verb "is". "Something" singular subject है, इसके साथ singular verb "is" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q5. Look at those boys; _______ section of the group is participating in the drill.
questions.explanation: Use "Every" as an adjective before the noun "section". "Every" यहाँ adjective का काम कर रहा है जो noun "section" से पहले आएगा।

questions.question: Q6. Each of the participants _______ given a certificate of appreciation.
questions.explanation: "Each of" is followed by a plural noun but takes a singular verb. "Each of" के बाद noun plural होता है पर verb singular "is" आता है।

questions.question: Q7. Every student in the class _______ to excel in the upcoming examinations.
questions.explanation: "Every" followed by a singular noun takes a singular verb "wishes". "Every" के साथ singular noun और singular verb "wishes" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q8. Mr. Khanna has two daughters, but _______ of them is interested in music.
questions.explanation: "Neither" is used for choosing between two persons in a negative sense. दो व्यक्तियों के लिए negative sense में "neither" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q9. You can take _______ of the five books kept on the table.
questions.explanation: "Any" is used for more than two options in a positive sentence. दो से अधिक विकल्पों के लिए positive वाक्य में "any" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q10. Neither of the two candidates _______ selected for the final round.
questions.explanation: "Neither" refers to two people and always takes a singular verb. "Neither" दो व्यक्तियों के लिए use होता है और इसके साथ singular verb "was" आता है।

questions.question: Q11. Both the manager _______ the supervisor were present at the site.
questions.explanation: "Both" is always followed by the conjunction "and". "Both" के साथ हमेशा conjunction "and" का प्रयोग किया जाता है, "as well as" का नहीं।

questions.question: Q12. Neither the players nor the coach _______ happy with the decision.
questions.explanation: When subjects are joined by "neither-nor", the verb agrees with the nearest subject "coach". "Neither-nor" में verb पास वाले subject "coach" के अनुसार singular होगा।

questions.question: Q13. Not only the principal but also the teachers _______ attending the meeting.
questions.explanation: The verb follows the nearest subject "teachers", which is plural. "Not only-but also" में verb पास वाले subject "teachers" के अनुसार plural (were) होगा।

questions.question: Q14. Either you or your brother _______ to take responsibility for this mistake.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the nearer subject "brother", which is singular. Verb अपने सबसे पास वाले subject "brother" के अनुसार singular "has" होगा।

questions.question: Q15. Neither the size nor the colors of the dress _______ me.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with "colors", which is the nearest plural subject. Verb पास वाले subject "colors" (plural) के अनुसार plural "suit" होगा।

questions.question: Q16. She donated five _______ rupees to the orphanage last month.
questions.explanation: "Thousand" acts as an adjective here and cannot be pluralized. यहाँ "thousand" adjective का काम कर रहा है, इसलिए इसका plural नहीं बनेगा।

questions.question: Q17. The merchant bought several _______ of eggs for the bakery.
questions.explanation: "Dozens" acts as a noun here and can be pluralized. यहाँ "dozens" noun का काम कर रहा है, इसलिए इसका plural रूप "dozens" सही है।

questions.question: Q18. Two _______ people attended the international seminar yesterday.
questions.explanation: "Thousand" is an adjective for the noun "people" and stays singular. "Thousand" यहाँ "people" के लिए adjective है, इसलिए यह singular रहेगा।

questions.question: Q19. He gave me three _______ pencils to complete the drawing.
questions.explanation: When preceded by a number, words like dozen stay singular as adjectives. संख्या होने पर dozen adjective का काम करता है और singular रहता है।

questions.question: Q20. _______ of books were distributed among the poor children.
questions.explanation: "Thousands" acts as a noun before "of" and is plural. "Of" से पहले "thousands" noun का काम कर रहा है, इसलिए plural रूप सही है।

questions.question: Q21. I have a few doubts _______ he will be able to finish the task.
questions.explanation: Use "whether" when there is uncertainty or doubt. जब संदेह (doubt) या अनिश्चितता हो, तब "whether" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q22. I know that _______ he works hard, he will pass the exam.
questions.explanation: "If" is used here to indicate a condition, which is grammatically correct. यहाँ "if" condition (शर्त) बता रहा है, जो व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है।

questions.question: Q23. He is _______ than his elder brother in many aspects.
questions.explanation: Avoid double comparatives like "more wiser"; "wiser" is sufficient. दो comparatives (more और wiser) एक साथ नहीं आते; केवल "wiser" सही है।

questions.question: Q24. Sachin is capable of doing _______ work if he stays focused.
questions.explanation: "Better" can take "much" but never "more" before it. "Better" के साथ "much" लग सकता है लेकिन "more" का प्रयोग गलत है।

questions.question: Q25. The weather today is _______ hot compared to yesterday.
questions.explanation: "Comparatively" is used with a positive degree adjective "hot". "Comparatively" के साथ positive degree adjective "hot" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q26. Earlier job opportunities were _______ good for freshers.
questions.explanation: "Relatively" takes a positive degree adjective "good". "Relatively" के साथ positive degree adjective "good" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q27. It does more harm than _______ in the long run.
questions.explanation: After "than", use a positive degree when comparing effects. "Than" के बाद यहाँ positive degree "good" का प्रयोग सही है।

questions.question: Q28. He is twice _______ his brother in terms of height.
questions.explanation: With words like "twice", use "as - as" for comparison. "Twice" जैसे शब्दों के साथ comparison के लिए "as - as" का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Q29. How _______ milk and how many eggs do you need?
questions.explanation: "Much" is used for uncountable nouns like "milk". Uncountable noun "milk" के साथ "much" का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

questions.question: Q30. _______ mangoes are ripe and ready to be eaten.
questions.explanation: "All these" is the correct order as demonstrative adjectives follow "all". "All these" सही क्रम है क्योंकि demonstrative adjective "all" के बाद आता है।

questions.question: Q31. She is _______ than beautiful, which helps her in her career.
questions.explanation: When comparing two qualities of the same person, use "more + positive degree". एक ही व्यक्ति की दो खूबियों की तुलना में "more" का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Q32. He is _______ than wise, often taking risks without thinking.
questions.explanation: Use "more brave" instead of "braver" when comparing two qualities of one person. एक व्यक्ति की दो खूबियों की तुलना के लिए "more brave" का प्रयोग करें।

questions.question: Q33. The box of Nestle’s chocolates _______ missing from the counter.
questions.explanation: The main subject "box" is singular, so the verb is "is". Main subject "box" singular है, इसलिए singular verb "is" लगेगा।

questions.question: Q34. The boxes of imported chocolates _______ arriving tomorrow.
questions.explanation: The main subject "boxes" is plural, so it takes the plural verb "are". Main subject "boxes" plural है, इसलिए plural verb "are" लगेगा।

questions.question: Q35. A flock of birds _______ flying over the lake at sunset.
questions.explanation: "A flock" is a singular collective noun, requiring the singular verb "was". "A flock" singular collective noun है, इसलिए singular verb "was" लगेगा।

questions.question: Q36. The prices of tomatoes _______ soared in the last week.
questions.explanation: The main subject is "prices" (plural), so use the plural verb "have". Main subject "prices" plural है, इसलिए plural verb "have" का प्रयोग होगा।

questions.question: Q37. One-third of the pillar _______ rotted away due to moisture.
questions.explanation: For fractions, the verb depends on the noun "pillar" (singular). Fraction के बाद verb noun "pillar" (singular) के अनुसार "has" होगा।

questions.question: Q38. Two-thirds of the pillars _______ been damaged in the storm.
questions.explanation: "Pillars" is plural, so "two-thirds" takes the plural verb "have". "Pillars" plural है, इसलिए "two-thirds" के साथ plural verb "have" लगेगा।

questions.question: Q39. Many a girl _______ to college by bus every day.
questions.explanation: "Many a" is followed by a singular noun and a singular verb. "Many a" के बाद singular noun और singular verb "goes" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q40. A great many students _______ declared successful this year.
questions.explanation: "A great many" is always followed by a plural noun and plural verb. "A great many" के साथ plural noun और plural verb "have been" आता है।

questions.question: Q41. More than one room _______ vacant in the hotel.
questions.explanation: "More than one" takes a singular noun and singular verb. "More than one" के साथ singular noun और singular verb "is" लगता है।

questions.question: Q42. More than two rooms _______ available for the guests.
questions.explanation: "More than two" is followed by a plural noun and plural verb. "More than two" के साथ plural noun और plural verb "are" आता है।

questions.question: Q43. More rooms than one _______ vacant for the tourists.
questions.explanation: When "rooms" comes before "than one", the verb is plural. जब "rooms" "than one" से पहले आता है, तब plural verb "are" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q44. The number of orders received today _______ very small.
questions.explanation: "The number of" takes a plural noun but a singular verb. "The number of" के साथ plural noun लेकिन singular verb "is" का प्रयोग होता है।

questions.question: Q45. Mr. Sharma is one of our officers who _______ always alert.
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun "who" refers to the plural "officers". Relative pronoun "who" plural "officers" को refer करता है, इसलिए verb "are" होगा।

questions.question: Q46. Mr. Sharma is the only one of our officers that _______ always alert.
questions.explanation: "The only one" emphasizes a single person, taking a singular verb. "The only one" केवल एक व्यक्ति पर जोर देता है, इसलिए singular verb "is" आएगा।

questions.question: Q47. It is I who _______ responsible for the delay in the project.
questions.explanation: The verb follows the antecedent of "who", which is "I". "Who" से पहले आने वाले subject "I" के अनुसार verb "am" होगा।

questions.question: Q48. He is one of the best writers that _______ ever lived.
questions.explanation: "That" refers to the plural noun "writers", so use the plural verb "have". "That" plural noun "writers" के लिए है, इसलिए plural verb "have" लगेगा।

questions.question: Q49. If you _______ hard, you will definitely pass the exam.
questions.explanation: In conditional sentences, the "if" clause is in Simple Present. Conditional sentence में "if" वाला भाग Simple Present (work) में होता है।

questions.question: Q50. If he _______ to me, I would have helped him.
questions.explanation: For past unfulfilled conditions, use "had + V3" in the if-clause. पुरानी अधूरी शर्त के लिए "if" clause में "had + V3" (had come) का प्रयोग करें।
